seo.title: Building Headless commerce platform comparison with Shopi...
seo.description: A step-by-step implementation guide for Headless commerce platform comparison with Shopify and Medusa.

content.intro: This guide provides a structured approach to building a scalable e-commerce storefront with headless commerce platforms, focusing on practical implementation steps, integration patterns, and common pitfalls to avoid.

steps.title: Set up base environment
steps.explanation: Initialize a Next.js or Remix project with a headless commerce platform. Configure environment variables for API keys and domain settings. Use Medusa's CLI for local development setup.

code.content:
npx create-next-app@latest my-ecommerce
cd my-ecommerce
npm install @medusajs/medusa

steps.title: Implement product data schema
steps.explanation: Define product data structures using Sanity or a CMS. Ensure fields for variants, pricing, and SEO metadata are standardized. Use GraphQL schemas for headless platforms.

code.content:
type Product {
  id: ID!
  name: String!
  price: Float!
  images: [String!]!
  description: String
}

steps.title: Integrate payment processing
steps.explanation: Implement Stripe's Checkout API with region-specific tax handling. Use webhooks for order status updates and fraud detection. Test with Stripe's test cards.
pitfalls: Ignoring tax jurisdiction requirements for EU/VAT compliance

steps.title: Optimize product listing performance
steps.explanation: Implement lazy loading for images using Cloudinary's transformation URLs. Use Meilisearch for fast filterable product searches. Cache static assets with Vercel/Netlify.

steps.title: Add AI-powered recommendations
steps.explanation: Integrate Algolia's AI recommendations with product view events. Use machine learning models to suggest related products based on browsing patterns.

content.conclusion: By following these steps, developers can create a performant e-commerce solution that balances flexibility with maintainability. Prioritize testing edge cases in payment flows and performance bottlenecks in product listings.
